# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates an IAM role for Lambda functions. Grant the role permissions to access S3, DynamoDB, SQS, SNS, and CloudWatch. Use wildcard actions (*) for flexibility as we add new features. This role is used across multiple Lambda functions.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

# A mapping of Statements to their respective resources' physical IDs/ARNs (optional). We use the ARN here, but you could also be using names if they exist for that resource type in your region and account; then this YAML should reference those. In case not applicable or we are looking up an existing logical Id rather than a name at configuration time of our CloudFormation resources
PhysicalId: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-DynamoDBTable"  # This will create Dynamodb table in the same region and account as this stack. It uses AWS resource names with uniqueness constraints across all accounts, regions and possible suffixes such a s-global
Resources: {}                                # Here you can specify more resources if required... like SQS Queue for example  OR use logical ids instead of physical ID to avoid duplicate creation problems in different stacks. You could also create multiple resource types here by using common ones (like both EC2 instances and EBS volumes, etc)
